rules.text: Games & Scoring
In league matches, every team will play with all except one team. Each team gets to play a total for 3 matches
Each league match will have 3 sets, with first 2 sets of 21 points and 3rd set of 15 points
3rd set in league match is played for 15 points only if first two sets are split between the 2 teams
The tie breaker for 21 points will go till 23 points and 15 points will go till 18 points. i.e. whoever reaches 23/18 points first will win
The top 4 seeds will play in the semi-finals. Seed 1 v. Seed 4 and Seed 2 v. Seed 3
All sets in Semis and Finals are played for 25 points (cap at 28) - IF we can start Semis on time and 3rd set of 15 points
3rd set in semis and finals match is played for 15 points only if first two sets are split between the 2 teams
There will be 1 timeout allowed per set per team for 1 minute
Points Calculation
Winning team will get 3 points for a match
3 Bonus points are awared if a team wins in 2 straight sets
2 Bonus points are awarded if team wins in 3 sets
1 point is awarded to losing team if the team wins a set
Ex: A team can win max of 6 points per match and 5 points min per match won
Determining Seeds
The seeds (semi-finalists) are determined based on:
Number of points won
In case of a equal number of matches won, the team with larger difference of points for sets which they won the matches will be higher seed
In case of equal difference of points the head-to-head will be seen and team that won the head-to-head will be higher seed
Play Rules
All players must rotate for every change in serve, including the 1st serve. i.e. player who is serving within team changes, team must rotate.
Teams swap sides of court at the start of 2nd set and after 7 points in 3rd set.
The players must maintain the positions before the serve and can make changes afterwards.
Player may not hit the ball twice in succession. (A block is not considered a hit.)
Maximum of 3 hits per side
Ball may be played off the net during a volley and on a serve.
A ball hitting a boundary line is in.
A ball is out if it hits an antennae, the floor completely outside the court, any of the net or cables outside the antennae, the referee stand or pole, the ceiling above a non-playable area.

It is legal to contact the ball with any part of a player’s body. It may happen all 3 times during the 3 hits while playing on one side. It is illegal if the ball rolls or contacts more than once on player's body (except when receiving a spike)
A player cannot block or attack a serve from on or inside the 10-foot line.
Violations during play resulting in a point to opponent
When serving, the player steps on or across the service line as while making contact with the ball.
Failure to serve the ball over the net successfully.
Ball-handling errors. Contacting the ball illegally (double touching, lifting, carrying, throwing, etc.)
Touching the net with any part of the body while the ball is in play.

When blocking a ball coming from the opponent’s court, it’s illegal to contact the ball when reaching over the net if both your opponent has not used 3 contacts AND they have a player there to make a play on the ball.
When attacking a ball coming from the opponent’s court, contacting the ball when reaching over the net is a violation if the ball has not yet broken the vertical plane of the net.
Crossing the court centerline with any part of your body is a violation. The umpire's call is FINAL, but if there is any sort of disturbance to opponent player its called fault.
Serving out of rotation/order.

Back-row player blocking (deflecting a ball coming from the opponent) when, at the moment of contact, the back-row player is near the net and has part of his/her body above the top of the net. This is an illegal block.
Back-row player attacking a ball inside the front zone (the area inside the 3M/10-foot line) when, at the moment of contact, the ball is completely above the net. This is an illegal attack.
Challenges
Challenge has to be done by captain. and umpire decides if he wants to revert the call and if call is reverted the point is replayed.
If any other player other than captain argues with umpire or delays the game for more than 30 seconds, the team will lose their timeout first and then a point subsequently

Umpire's call is final on any call/challenge by Captains. If there confusion and umpire/refree(s) are unable to take decision on a critical challenge, it will be discussed with organizers and decision will be made with the help of organizing team.
Rules on Rotation
If the team plays 7, they must play all 7 (with one rotation) throughout including the finals.
Each team must provide their rotation with all players on the team to refree/umpire before the game begins. Once provided, the rotation cannot be altered with-in the game.

Ghost rule: During/on tournament day(s), if a player is injured, or have to leave early or come late due to other commitments, that team shall play with 5 (team with 6 players) or 6 (team with 7 players), but with ghost rotation (assuming that there is invisible player/ghost is in the missing player's place), the rotation must be followed under any circumstances. However, the player can join back anytime.
For the serve during ghost rotation, either injured player can serve and stay out, or, center-back (#6 player) in the rotation shall serve.
Semis and Finals
The finals is played for 3 sets (best of 3) with all three sets played for 25 points and 28 points maximum, in case of tie breaker
Each team will get 2 timeouts per set per team for 1 minute.
All the rules will apply to finals as well when it comes to violations and play.
